---
title: "Field Solvers"
description: "Connect a field-solver definition to the tracked beam."
audience: user
status: planned
last-reviewed: "2026-07-22"
---

`FIELDSOLVER` is registered by the current OPALX executable and is selected by
the tracking `RUN` statement. Solver choices control mesh dimensions, boundary
conditions, decomposition, Green functions, and optional debugging output.

The complete attribute matrix is not yet verified for every execution backend.
Until it is published here:

- Start from a passing regression case using the intended solver.
- Preserve its boundary-condition and parallel-decomposition settings.
- Change one solver parameter at a time.
- Check mesh and time-step convergence.
- Confirm GPU support for any newly selected algorithm.

The [Beam-Beam](../physics/beam-beam/index.qmd) chapter contains a documented
open-boundary manufactured solution. For beams with a large longitudinal
energy or velocity spread, see [Structures / Binning](structures/index.qmd#binning)
for the source-audited `BINNING` command and `FIELDSOLVER.BINS` connection.
